decisions.title: Add complexity_coverage as 4th MTI component (weight 0.15)
decisions.context: Three-component MTI (coverage=0.40, evidence=0.20, consistency=0.25) did not reward stories that touched complex endpoints or containers. High-complexity work was underweighted relative to trivial stories.
decisions.decision: Add complexity_coverage (weight 0.15) sourced from veritas enrich.js enrichment step. Rebalance weights: coverage=0.40, evidence=0.20, consistency=0.25, complexity=0.15. Falls back to 3-component formula when enrichment data is absent (backwards compat).
decisions.consequences: Stories lacking story_ids on endpoints will score 0 for complexity_coverage. This creates an incentive to stamp story_ids (F37-10-001). veritas audit pipeline now runs enrich() between reconcile() and computeTrust().
alternatives_considered: Keep 3-component formula: rejected -- does not differentiate complex vs trivial work
Replace consistency with complexity: rejected -- consistency catches naming drift which is valuable
decisions.notes: Activated when story_ids stamped on endpoints. Currently falls back to 3-component (MTI=86 local).

decisions.title: HTTP API is the sole agent interface -- no direct file reads of model/*.json
decisions.context: AI agents (Copilot, chain-of-thought loops) were reading model/*.json source files directly, resulting in 10-15 tool calls per context-gathering task. The files are implementation details of the API server and may be stale.
decisions.decision: Agents must use the HTTP API exclusively. model/*.json files are write-only artifacts produced by POST /model/admin/export. The copilot-instructions GOLDEN RULE enforces this: agents that grep model files are forbidden.
decisions.consequences: Agents need to know the ACA base URL. One HTTP call replaces 10 file reads. Model files may lag Cosmos by up to 60s (cache TTL) but are authoritative at export time.
alternatives_considered: Direct file reads: rejected -- stale, bypasses row_version, no audit trail
SDK/library: rejected -- overhead for PowerShell and CLI agents
decisions.notes: Documented in copilot-instructions.md GOLDEN RULE section and USER-GUIDE.md.

decisions.title: Use IFPUG FPA (UFP method) for story-point estimation
decisions.context: The team needed a reproducible, model-driven story-point estimation method. COSMIC, USE CASE POINTS, and IFPUG FPA were evaluated. The model already tracks containers (ILF/EIF candidates) and endpoints (EI/EO/EQ).
decisions.decision: Use IFPUG Function Point Analysis. Containers map to data function types (ILF/EIF). Endpoints map to transaction function types (EI/EO/EQ). UFP = sum of weighted components. Story points = UFP * 2.4 (COCOMO II). Effort-days = UFP * 0.5.
decisions.consequences: FP accuracy depends on DET/FTR counts per endpoint. Initial estimates use default complexity weights. As det_count/ftr_count fields are populated, accuracy improves. GET /model/fp/estimate returns live UFP.
alternatives_considered: COSMIC: rejected -- requires operational process mapping not available in current model
Story poker only: rejected -- no reproducibility, no model linkage
Use Case Points: rejected -- no use case catalog in model
decisions.notes: fp/estimate requires ACA redeploy to activate (DPDCA sprint image). TFT: EI=23 EO=25 EQ=28. DFT: ILF=12 EIF=1.

decisions.title: Use Cosmos DB NoSQL as the primary persistence layer
decisions.context: The data model needs a persistence layer that is 24x7 available, schemaless to accommodate evolving layers, and accessible from ACA without VNet dependencies. Options considered: PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Azure Table Storage, Cosmos DB NoSQL.
decisions.decision: Use Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL API (single container model_objects, partition key /layer) hosted on marco-sandbox-cosmos. ACA Container App reads COSMOS_URL + COSMOS_KEY from environment secrets.
decisions.consequences: All 31 layers stored in one Cosmos container. Partition key /layer enables efficient per-layer queries. RU cost scales with object count. Single-worker constraint on ACA avoids split-brain with MemoryStore.
alternatives_considered: PostgreSQL Flexible Server: rejected -- requires VNet peering or public endpoint, higher operational overhead
Azure Table Storage: rejected -- no JSON-native query, poor cross-layer graph traversal
In-memory only: rejected -- data lost on ACA restart
